channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An Israeli strike in Gaza kills World Central Kitchen workers. Israel says 1 was an Oct. 7 attacker
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a car in the Gaza Strip on Saturday killed five people, including employees of World Central Kitchen.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, TIA GOLDENBERG and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump wants pardoned real estate developer Charles Kushner to become US ambassador to France
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday he intends to nominate real estate developer Charles Kushner, father of Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, to serve as ambassador to France.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: (WGHP) — Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44. According to TMZ, Bryar was found dead in his Tennessee home Tuesday after last being seen alive on November 4.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, ABC 4

item.title: Canada's Trudeau returns home after Trump meeting without assurances that tariffs are off the table
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau returned home Saturday after his meeting with Donald Trump without assurances the president-elect will back away from threatened tariffs on all products from the major American trading partner.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hidden gems of early Cyber Monday deals
item.description: BestReviews has assembled a collection of top-quality gifts priced under $50, currently available at discounts of up to 60% off.
item.dc:creator: Lizzy Briskin, ABC 4

item.title: Musk, Stallone among star-studded names partying at Trump's Mar-a-Lago club for Thanksgiving
item.description: A number of high profile names descended on Mar-a-Lago this thanksgiving including tech billionaire Elon Musk, “Rocky" star Sylvester Stallone and Canadian Prime Minister Just Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania restaurant ordered to pay $184,000 for withholding tips
item.description: The U.S. Department of Labor says Sly Fox Brewing Company in Pittsburgh "unlawfully kept a portion of the tips earned by workers, allowing managers to participate in the restaurant’s tip pool.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Earl Holliman, 'The Rainmaker' and 'Twilight Zone' actor, dead at 96
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Award-winning actor Earl Holliman has died at the age of 96, according to multiple reports. Holliman died Monday in hospice care at his home in Studio City according to his spouse, Craig Curtis, who told The Hollywood Reporter.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KREX 5

item.title: Trump and Republicans in Congress eye an ambitious 100-day agenda, starting with tax cuts
item.description: Republicans swept to power on Election Day and now control the House, the Senate and the White House, with plans for an ambitious 100-day agenda come January.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why your favorite catalogs are smaller this holiday season
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Honey, they shrunk the catalogs. While retailers hope to go big this holiday season, customers may notice that the printed gift guides arriving in their mailboxes are smaller. Many of the millions of catalogs getting sent to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LIVE: Lake Effect Snow Warning extended; emergencies declared and highways closed
item.description: (WJET/WFXP) — As we enter into the weekend, many people are waking up to 1-3 feet of snow with buried cars and snow-covered roadways. Travel concerns are still very prevalent, with the continued closure of major highways and roads with heavy accumulation.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the plastic pollution crisis as treaty talks conclude in South Korea
item.description: The world’s nations will wrap up negotiating a treaty this weekend to address the global crisis of plastic pollution.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DNA test brings New York father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives. For one family in Rockland County, this is a special holiday. A daughter and her biological father are together.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, KTLA 5

item.title: Easy thanksgiving leftovers recipes, ideas to reduce your food waste
item.description: Thanksgiving leftover recipes include treats, soups, and turkey broth. Other common ideas include salads, sandwiches, pies, and casseroles.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, NewsNation

item.title: Lake-effect snow heading for New York state could disrupt travel and football
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — Black Friday was giving way to a white weekend in parts of New York state, with the first big snow of the season threatening to bury towns along lakes Erie and Ontario during a hectic holiday travel and shopping weekend.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Auto industry may lose 17 percent of profits due to Trump’s tariff plan: Report
item.description: Carmakers in the U.S. and Europe could lose up to 17 percent of their combined annual core profits in a worst-case scenario if the U.S. imposes steep tariffs on Europe, and two key U.S. allies, Mexico and Canada, a report from S&P Global released Friday showed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Colorado officer uses kid's bike during pursuit
item.description: A Lone Tree police officer went above and beyond to try and catch a theft suspect who fled on a scooter in a neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, KRON4

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus after disastrous Thanksgiving loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Hot showers can harm skin, hair health, dermatologists say
item.description: Scalding showers can lead to the removal of skin layers and essential oils from your body, which can be damaging.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, ABC 4

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, NewsNation

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives. For one family in Rockland County, this is a special holiday. A daughter and her biological father are together.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ranking the Democrats: Here's who the party could nominate next as president
item.description: Democrats are licking their wounds after Vice President Harris’s defeat to President-elect Trump, but already are looking toward who might lead their party in a 2028 presidential contest. It’s a fight that looks wide open.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Donald Trump's call for 'energy dominance' is likely to run into real-world limits
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to create a National Energy Council that he says will establish American “energy dominance” around the world as he seeks to boost U.S. oil and gas drilling and move away from President Joe Biden’s focus on climate change.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida man dies after getting knocked off boat, caught in propeller
item.description: A Florida man died during a fishing trip with a friend after a mishap landed both men in the water, ultimately causing him to be run over by the vessel and caught in its motor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Child's toes 'severed' by lawnmower at Utah daycare; state wants it to shut down
item.description: A childcare center in southern Utah is losing its license and must cease operations by Dec. 4 after a child's toes were "severed" by a lawnmower.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KTLA 5

item.title: Sotheby's settles with New York for $6.25M over tax fraud
item.description: The iconic New York City auction house Sotheby's agreed to pay over $6 for allegedly helping clients avoid millions in sales tax.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Why retailers still see Black Friday as the high point of the holiday shopping season
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After weeks of plugging increasingly inviting discounts, retailers in the United States and several other countries are preparing for prime time: Black Friday, the bargain bonanza that still reigns as the unofficial kickoff of the holiday shopping season even if it's lost some luster
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: 100 years of the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
item.description: The parade in New York City, presented by department store chain Macy's, was first held in 1924 under the heading "Macy's Christmas Parade" to promote holiday sales and spotlight the newly expanded and, at the time, largest in the world Herald Square store in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Caldwell, Stacker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 injured after plane crashes into tree during emergency landing in California
item.description: Two people were injured Monday afternoon after a plane was forced to make an emergency landing and struck a tree.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to avoid starting 'phantom traffic jams' this holiday travel season
item.description: Bumper-to-bumper traffic is a regular occurrence for holiday travelers. However, researchers with Vanderbilt University hope findings from a large traffic study can help.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Formula 1 expands grid to add General Motors' Cadillac brand and new American team for 2026 season
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Formula 1 on Monday at last said it will expand its grid in 2026 to make room for an American team that is partnered with General Motors. “As the pinnacle of motorsports, F1 demands boundary-pushing innovation and excellence.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5
